expectations.text: Describes the KYC-SA (Know Your Customer – Security Attestation) application on swift.com as the submission channel and explains the annual attestation cycle
expectations.evidence: "Submission is via the SWIFT portal at **swift.com/myswift**." Also discusses the annual cycle: "Under CSCF v2026, SWIFT has opened an extended attestation window: July 1 – December 31, 2026" and "Next cycle | CSCF v2027 is expected to be published July 2026 — plan for a fresh control review ahead of the following attestation cycle".

expectations.text: States the independent assessment requirement: the attestation must be supported by an independent assessment performed by either an external assessment provider or an independent internal function (e.g., second/third line of defence), per the CSP Independent Assessment Framework
expectations.evidence: "Since CSCF v2020, self-attestation is no longer sufficient — an independent assessment is mandatory for all users, including A1 institutions... Eligible assessors: Your internal audit team — permitted, but only if sufficiently independent... An external audit firm with demonstrated SWIFT CSP assessment competency... SWIFT-certified assessors listed on SWIFT's KYC Registry." This covers external or independent internal function, though it does not name the 'CSP Independent Assessment Framework' explicitly.

expectations.text: Notes assessor independence and competence requirements (assessors must not have implemented or operated the controls they assess; relevant security assessment experience/certifications)
expectations.evidence: "The assessor must not have operational responsibility for the SWIFT environment being assessed. If your internal audit function reports into the same chain as SWIFT operations, or has previously configured/managed SWIFT infrastructure, they are disqualified" plus references to 'demonstrated SWIFT CSP assessment competency' and 'SWIFT-certified assessors'.

expectations.text: Describes preparation: confirming scope for architecture type A1, collecting evidence per control, and completing control-by-control compliance status in the KYC-SA form
expectations.evidence: "Confirm A1 architecture scope with your SWIFT relationship manager/internal architecture owner" plus a full per-control evidence checklist table and "Per-control attestation: For each of the mandatory controls applicable to A1, attest one of: Implemented / Partially Implemented / Not Implemented".

expectations.text: Explains post-submission consequences: counterparties can request and view attestation data via KYC-SA for counterparty risk decisions, and SWIFT may report non-compliant or non-attesting users to local financial supervisors
expectations.evidence: Response states counterparty visibility ("your correspondent banks and counterparties can view your attestation status right away") but never states that SWIFT itself reports non-compliant or non-attesting users to local financial supervisors — it only vaguely notes 'regulators may reference your attestation status' and ties compliance to general supervisory expectations (DORA, NY DFS, MAS TRM), which is not the same specific claim.
